facts: At light speed, you'd reach Mars in 3 minutes and 20 seconds
Traveling at the speed of the fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, it would take 70,000 years to reach Mars
If you drove to Mars at 60 mph, it would take 340,000,000 hours or around 41,000 years, which is like driving from New York to Los Angeles over 14,500 times
idea.payoff: and we still can't make it happen
idea.title: Mars in 3 mins? 🚀
idea.description: Imagine commuting to Mars in just a few minutes 🚀💫, but the reality is, even at high speeds, it's a journey that's still beyond our reach 🌌. What if you could travel at the speed of light? 🤔 You'd be sipping coffee on the Red Planet before you know it, but for now, it's just a dream 🌟. Can humans ever make this cosmic commute a reality?

scenes.visual: A visually striking animation of Earth and Mars orbiting, with a futuristic question mark appearing dramatically between them.

scenes.text:
At light speed:
3 min 20 sec to Mars
scenes.visual: Earth on the left, Mars on the right. A brilliant white beam of light instantly shoots from Earth to Mars, highlighting the path.
Earth on the left, Mars on the right. A tiny spacecraft icon slowly crawls a minuscule distance across the screen, emphasizing the vastness and time scale.

scenes.text:
Driving 60 mph:
41,000 YEARS to Mars
(Like 14.5k NY-LA trips!)
scenes.visual: Earth on the left, Mars on the right. A small car icon attempts to drive along a ridiculously long, winding road that stretches across the screen, a tiny progress bar fills minutely.

scenes.text:
And we still can't
make it happen.
scenes.visual: Earth and Mars are shown as small, distant points in an immense, dark, star-filled void, emphasizing the overwhelming scale and challenge. A subtle 'sigh' or 'thought bubble' graphic appears over Earth.
